metadata.title: One Logo, 47 Paths, Every Move Remotion Knows.
metadata.description: A technique-showcase reel animating the Musinique logo every way Remotion can — 9:16 portrait. One near-square SVG (1698×1832), 47 individually addressable paths, 20 techniques. Which moves deserve to live?

beats.narration_text: Hey, this is Liam, in for Bear. One logo. Forty-seven paths. We're going to run the Musinique mark through every motion move Remotion knows, back to back, so you can judge which ones actually deserve to live.
Spring Entrance. The whole mark pops in as one unit on a default spring. Clean, direct, nothing fancy. Judge the snap.
Overshoot Spring. High-bounce config, squash on landing, stretch on rise. Same spring, different mass and damping. Watch whether the energy reads as playful or desperate.
Per-Path Stagger. All forty-seven paths enter in sequence, offset by four frames each. At this count, the cascade is rich enough to feel like assembly rather than delay.
Draw-On Stroke. evolvePath traces the outline on a selection of paths, then the fill floods in. The corner fidelity here depends entirely on path complexity.
Mask Reveal. Clip-path wipes left to right, then an iris closes from center. Two directions, one beat. The iris is the move you never see coming.
Scale Zoom. Push in from eight times normal down to fit. Linear run first, then the bezier version. One easing function is a camera move, the other is math.
Rotation. Pivot entrance, then a slow continuous hold. The mark is near-square, so rotation works differently here than on wide wordmarks.
Skew And Shear. Italic lean-in, hold, then release back to vertical. Whether it reads as dynamic or distorted depends entirely on the degree.
Opacity Through Blur. Fade-through-blur entrance — the mark materializes out of soft focus. Subtle. If you have to look for it, the blur is doing its job.
Color Interpolation. Ink sweeps to terracotta path by path, left to right, then back. This is a treatment beat — the palette violation is intentional and temporary.
Kinetic Scatter. All forty-seven paths explode outward from center, then spring back to their positions. Chaos to order. Watch whether the snap reads as resolution or collapse.
Glitch Slices. Horizontal slices offset across the mark for about eight frames, then snap clean. The resolution should feel like a correction, not a decoration.
Trail Echo. Low-opacity copies follow a fast slide across the frame, then the mark settles. Motion blur you can count.
Noise Wobble. Noise-driven micro-jitter across the paths, then it settles to stillness. The question is whether it reads as alive or broken.
Elastic Physics. Drop-in with scaleY squash and rebound. Spring config is everything here — too stiff and it reads hard, too loose and it reads cheap.
Path Cascade. The forty-seven paths rain in from above in a staggered waterfall. With this many elements, the cascade has texture you won't get from a wordmark.
Card Flip. Perspective rotateY — the mark on both faces. The hold after the flip is where you decide whether the second face earns its frame time.
Shadow Play. The shadow animates independently — offset grows, blurs, then snaps back to join the mark. The brand's depth in one move.
Composer Summon. The mark appears to be sent from a Claude composer — terracotta spark fires on send, then the mark materializes. The brand's deepest trick.
Exit Family. Three quick exits back to back: shrink-spin, blur-out, mask-close. Which one you'd actually cut to black on — that is the judgment.
Now you have the receipts. Take this prompt, run it on your own SVG, and flag the two or three moves that actually serve the brand.
